learningCards.subject: Math
learningCards.topic: Plus and Minus in two-digital number
learningCards.parentSummary: In class we reviewed factoring patterns: pull out a GCF first, then try grouping or simple cases. At home, two textbook examples with clear intermediate steps are enough—no need to finish the whole set in one sitting.

tonightActions.text: Tonight: pick two homework items labeled “factoring” and rewrite each solution with one extra line of work shown.
Together, say aloud the first step you try before using a formula (GCF vs. pattern).
If it’s still stuck after 10 minutes, message the teacher with the problem number.

learningCards.subject: Art
learningCards.parentSummary: Horizon line and vanishing point—why hallways “feel” deep on paper.

tonightActions.text: Tonight: revisit “One-point perspective in drawing” with one class or homework example.
Ask your child to explain the main idea in their own words.
If it’s stuck after ~15 minutes, note the question and message the teacher.
